title: sophora tomentosa subsp. polyphylla
intro: Sophora tomentosa subsp. polyphylla, also known as the 'Koreanschwarze Soja' or 'Black Soybean Tree', is a plant species of the genus Sophora, characterized by its dark brown or black seeds and leaves that are used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, attributed to its high content of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Sophora tomentosa subsp. polyphylla include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used to make teas, capsules, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
